question_text: what is the minimum temperature that 6000-2RSH/GFJ can tolerate?
answer: Damage can result when the bearings are operated continuously below 10 °C. At the temperature of -40 °C, the torque required to start or run this bearing may be significantly higher. Below -40 °C is also not allowed for the seals used in this bearing.

question_text: what is the max temperature that 6206-2RS2/C3GJN can tolerate?
what is the minimum temperature that 6206-2RS2/C3GJN can tolerate?
answer: Damage can result when the bearings are operated continuously below 35 °C. At the temperature of -30 °C, the torque required to start or run this bearing may be significantly higher. Below -30 °C is also not allowed for the seals used in this bearing.

question_text: what is the max temperature that 6206-2RS2/C3S1GWP can tolerate?
answer: Temperatures above the 145 °C can be tolerated for short periods but may have an impact on the grease life. At 167 °C the grease used in this bearing will lose its structure and becomes a fluid.

question_text: what is the minimum temperature that 6206-2RS2/C3S1GWP can tolerate?
answer: Damage can result when the bearings are operated continuously below -22 °C. Below -30 °C is not allowed for the seals used in this bearing.

answer: If steel/steel combination the most common grease is LGHB 2, LGEP 2 or LGMT 3
